ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Gyoona ŋɔn daa bɛ zintitaari maa puu ni n‑niŋi daba ata ni yunsi ata maa, lala ka man Ninsaala Bii maa gba nin bɛ tingbaŋŋu puuni n‑niŋi daba ata ni yunsi ata bɛni.
translated: The one who is called Gyoona said that the people of Zintitaari should not go to the farm today because they will go to the farm tomorrow and also go to the market tomorrow.

ref: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
text: Ka daa gɔsi saazugu n‑nyurim u lɔŋŋu ni, ka yɛli doo maa, ni, “Efatta'a!” - dinŋɔ gbinni nyɛ la “Yoaama!”
translated: When he had spit on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and had anointed the eyes of the blind man, He said unto him, "Go, wash in the pool of Siloam," (which is by interpretation, Sent.

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Nyin birikɔnsi daana wa! Nyin daŋŋma n‑voaai i nimbiri ni zaantɔliga maa m‑basi ka nyari suŋŋa pɔi, ka naayi yiisi munseaa kan bɛ i mabii nimbiri ni!”
translated: You are welcome! You people should not be afraid. I have brought good news to you all, and I will tell you about the good things that will happen.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
text: Di saha maa ba zaa daa di n‑tigi, ka ba daa la'asi dikyɛba an kpalim la m‑paali peeri piiya ni ayi.
translated: When the sun sets, they say it is going to sleep, and it will wake up again tomorrow morning.

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: Ka ba daa paai u na ka wulisiri ban tum tumbɛseesi maa, ka u daa su ba tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi kɔsuubu, Gyɔdin mɔ'ari la ni.
translated: When he was a child, he used to go to the farm with his father, and he used to carry a small hoe, Gyɔdin's father said.
